instruction: I need you to write me an email to my wife's sister Sarah. The email is to let her know about our dinner next Monday at 7. The email must be written exclusively in English and include an English idiom. It should consist of at least six sentences, with the first sentence starting with the phrase "Dear Sarah" and the last sentence ending with the phrase "looking forward to it." The keyword "dinner" must appear exactly twice in the email. The tone should be warm and conversational, written in the first person, and include a brief mention of why the dinner is special. The email must include the phrase "The hosts request the pleasure of your company at this event."
llm_response: Dear Sarah, I hope you are having a wonderful week and that everything is going smoothly on your end. We are so excited to finally host you for a special dinner next Monday at 7, as this meal will be a celebration of our family coming together after such a busy year. The hosts request the pleasure of your company at this event, which we know will be a night filled with laughter and great stories. We have been working hard to make sure the dinner is perfect, and we truly believe it will be a night you will never forget. Please let us know if you can make it so we can finalize the menu, because we want this dinner to be absolutely memorable for everyone. We are looking forward to seeing you soon and can't wait to catch up, looking forward to it.
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The instruction contains a conflict between the requested tone ("warm and conversational, written in the first person") and the requirement to include the specific phrase "The hosts request the pleasure of your company at this event," which is highly formal and third-person. The model does not acknowledge this conflict; instead, it attempts to blend them into a single email. It also fails the constraint regarding the keyword "dinner," which appears three times in the response instead of exactly twice. Since the model did not identify or address the conflicting constraints and simply provided a response, it falls into category 1.

**Answer**: 1

Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and.new_constraint: The email must include the phrase "The hosts request the pleasure of your company at this event."
Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and.org_constraint: The tone should be warm and conversational, written in the first person.
